game_info.title: Bounce & Collect
game_info.concept: **Bounce and Collect:** Players initiate gameplay by tapping the top of the screen to drop a single ball into a vertically scrolling, physics-driven board filled with pegs and multiplier gates. The ball falls and bounces dynamically, aiming to pass through multiplier gates (e.g., x2, x3, x5) that duplicate the ball count before landing in collection bins at the bottom, each assigned a specific point value. Progression occurs across distinct levels, each featuring unique peg layouts and multiplier placements, where players aim to achieve a target score or collect a minimum number of balls within a set number of drops, typically 3-5 per level. Successfully completing levels unlocks new board layouts and potentially upgrades like starting with 2 balls or increasing multiplier effectiveness by 10%. The game ends when all drops are used, with the objective being to maximize the final score by strategically aiming drops to hit the highest multipliers.
game_info.description: Drop balls from the top of the screen to bounce through a peg-filled board, aiming to hit multiplier gates that duplicate your balls. Each ball lands in a collection bin at the bottom, earning points based on the bin's value. Complete levels by reaching target scores within limited drops. Strategic placement and timing are key to maximizing your score through multipliers!
game_info.controls:
LEFT ARROW: Move drop position left
RIGHT ARROW: Move drop position right
SPACE: Drop ball at current position
ENTER: Start game from start screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ball dropping, physics simulation, and collision detection with pegs. Verifying that balls bounce realistically and eventually land in collection bins. Ensures the core gameplay loop functions correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically drop balls at regular intervals from different horizontal positions. Allow balls to fall through the peg board and land in bins. Monitor that all balls eventually land and contribute to score.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All dropped balls should bounce off pegs following physics rules, pass through any multiplier gates encountered, and land in bottom bins. Score should increase appropriately. No balls should get stuck or disappear. Test passes when multiple drops complete successfully.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing multiplier gates functionality. Verifying that balls passing through multiplier gates correctly duplicate and that the duplicated balls behave as independent physics entities.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Target drops specifically at multiplier gate positions. Monitor the number of balls after passing through gates to ensure duplication occurs correctly (x2 creates 2 balls, x3 creates 3 balls, etc.).
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When a ball passes through a multiplier gate, it should duplicate into the appropriate number of balls. Each duplicate should have independent physics and contribute separately to the score when landing. Test passes when multipliers consistently work as expected.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing game over condition and drop limit. Verifying that the game correctly tracks remaining drops and transitions to game over when all drops are used without reaching the target score.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Use all available drops without attempting to maximize score. Intentionally avoid multiplier gates and aim for low-value bins. Monitor drop counter decrements.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Game should track drops correctly, show remaining drops, and transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all drops are exhausted without meeting the target score. Restart functionality should work from game over screen. Test passes when loss condition triggers appropriately.
TEST_4.description: Testing game over condition and drop limit. Verifying that the game correctly tracks remaining drops and transitions to game over when all drops are used without reaching the target score.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state preservation. Verifying that pausing stops physics simulation and gameplay, and unpausing resumes correctly without state corruption.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Drop balls and immediately pause mid-flight. Verify balls stop moving. Unpause and verify balls resume their physics simulation from the paused state. Repeat multiple times during different game states.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When paused, all balls and physics should freeze in place. When unpaused, gameplay should resume exactly where it left off. Score, drops remaining, and ball positions should be preserved. Test passes when pause/unpause cycles work flawlessly.
TEST_5.description: Testing pause functionality and game state preservation. Verifying that pausing stops physics simulation and gameplay, and unpausing resumes correctly without state corruption.

TEST_5.expected: When paused, all balls and physics should freeze in place. When unpaused, gameplay should resume exactly where it left off. Score, drops remaining, and ball positions should be preserved. Test passes when pause/unpause cycles work flawlessly.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing collision bin scoring system. Verifying that balls landing in different bins award the correct point values and that multiple balls in the same bin accumulate points properly.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Drop balls targeting specific bins with known point values. Track score increases to verify they match expected bin values. Test multiple balls landing simultaneously in the same bin.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each bin should award its designated point value when a ball lands in it. Multiple balls should each contribute their full value. Score display should update accurately. Test passes when all bins award correct points consistently.
TEST_6.description: Testing collision bin scoring system. Verifying that balls landing in different bins award the correct point values and that multiple balls in the same bin accumulate points properly.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions. Verifying proper behavior when balls interact with screen edges, multiple simultaneous collisions occur, and rapid input sequences are processed.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Game should handle edge cases gracefully without crashes or errors. Balls at screen edges should bounce correctly. Input validation should prevent invalid actions. Physics should remain stable during complex collision scenarios. Test passes when all edge cases are handled robustly.
